author: @koaasst
text:
so i gather, electrons are everywhere and they are always moving, and they like paths that are easy to travel, so we just jab electrodes everywhere we can think of to give them an easier avenue right into out gadget. the places they are more frequent travelers being better candidates, like as elecrolytes being a superhighway, compared to the pothole mountain pass of a big ole granite boulder. 
that word electro pops up alot, i guess i never just stopped and had a think about it, but its nice to have a comfy polishing of my mental image of a process.

author: @simonexperiences
text: did you try photomolecular evaporation with green light?

author: @SanctuaryGardenLiving
text: Children should be exposed to those gears.

author: @mattparker9726
text: 39:41  No I think of Tesla.  The man, not the car.  Robert builds a Tesla coil out of a microwave WHEN?

author: @mattparker9726
text: 30:32 hey who's that good looking chap, with the fancy haircut?  😋😋

author: @mattparker9726
text: 29:13  How is the aluminum oxide produced in the cell?  I suppose it's an aqueous solution?  I think the first step to selling this to the masses, would be an internal heating, or chemical desiccant that would turn it into a dry powder.  Preferably as simply as the device is itself.  Then said dried powder can be put into a collection vessel and shipped off.  As a non chemist, I would be much more inclined to handle a dried powder than some tincture of unknown toxicity.  Also, I don't see the link to the patent?  Do you have the number?

author: @mattparker9726
text: 16:34 HEY!  I have moss all in my woods around me!  You know, what about rotting wood?  Isn't there a bacterial component to that?  What about doing this with fungi?

author: @mattparker9726
text: 13:41 ok cool, how do I get one?  My tap water contains a large % of potassium.  (and probably fertilizer runoff, there's lots of farming here in MS.)

author: @mattparker9726
text: 8:21  What about chemical desiccants?  Like those little packets you get in food all the time that says "don't eat" all over it?

author: @mattparker9726
text: 6:47 I'd LOVE to do this experiment!  I am not a chemist though, and I noticed the name of the chemical had cyanide in the name.  Is there any way I could screw this up and poison myself?  Is there any off gassing from any of the reactants?  Just crossing my Ts.

author: @mattparker9726
text: 1:03  what I'm really interested in is a material, preferably a paint or varnish material, that can capture Alpha radiation.  (and transfer that directly into electricity  Obviously even a sheet of paper could stop it, but it wouldn't DO anything)  And it got me thinking about suspensions.  Specifically with that varnish idea, could you create a PN junction using a suspended reactant that could be cured and dried?  I suppose I could make a photonuclear cell with some radium tubelets basically taped to the panel, but A: that's been done before on YT, and B: we can't "legally"  get most radioactive materials here in the USA, thanks mommy Govt. And C: said tubes of radium would be HORRIFICALLY expensive to try and cover a 100W panel with, for a maximum output of around 33 watts.
